steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to compare the lengths of the two rivers — the Dnieper (2,287 km) and the Indus (3,610 km). The correct choice must use the length figures and place them in direct comparison.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The Indus River is in Asia, whereas the Dnieper River is located in Europe.': Compares locations (continents), not lengths. ✗
  • Choice B — 'The Dnieper River is shorter than the Indus River.': Directly compares the two rivers by length — 2,287 km vs. 3,610 km — which is exactly what the goal requires. ✓
  • Choice C — 'The Dnieper River in Europe is 2,287 kilometers long.': States only the Dnieper's length with no comparison to the Indus. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Among the longest rivers in the world, the Indus River is ranked No. 19.': States a world ranking for one river, not a length comparison between the two. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
